signals.title: JPY/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Intervention
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.081% vs USD 1W avg -0.880% (spread 1.961%) — JPY outperformance is critical following Japan’s $35B intervention which erased the FOMC-driven dollar rally. USD/JPY at 157.03 remains under pressure despite a 300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage as market participants price intervention risk over yield differentials. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) indicates a crowded short position vulnerable to further unwinds during Japan's Golden Week holidays.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7208 testing upper range as COT LF net long reaches +48,299 (74.8% long) while retail sentiment hits an extreme 90% short. This institutional vs retail divergence at the 0.7200 handle suggests retail is fading a fundamentally backed trend. RBA 4.10% vs Fed 3.75% provides a 35bp carry premium supporting the AUD bid. Retail capitulation risk is elevated if price sustains above 0.7210.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) shows speculators are heavily fading the RBNZ despite a hawkish market bias. With the RBNZ on hold at 2.25% but a hike bias persisting, the pair faces significant short-squeeze risk if the May 27 meeting confirms tightening. NZD/USD at 0.5900 is currently supported by this positioning imbalance despite the 150bp carry deficit vs USD.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + G8 Laggard
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.727 vs 252d norm of -0.138 (z=+1.89σ) — the pair is trading as a pure risk proxy rather than reflecting its 200bp carry disadvantage vs USD. EUR 1W avg -0.529% confirms broad Euro weakness as the second-worst G8 performer. Watch for a return to mean correlation or a break of 1.1700 to signal a shift back to carry-driven pricing.
